changes_made: Removed k8s_networkpolicy_change_management_approval_required as it does not align with NetworkPolicy's purpose
review_notes: The control CC8.1 is applicable to Kubernetes as it involves managing changes to infrastructure and configurations, which can be controlled through RBAC, admission controllers, and audit logging. However, NetworkPolicies are not suitable for managing change approvals, so that function was removed. The remaining functions are well-named and map correctly to Kubernetes security controls.
technical_concerns: NetworkPolicy is not used for change management approvals.
title: CC8.1 The entity authorizes, designs, develops or acquires, configures, documents, tests, approves, and implements changes to infrastructure, data, software, and procedures to meet its objectives
step1_reasoning: The control CC8.1 focuses on the authorization, design, development, configuration, documentation, testing, approval, and implementation of changes to infrastructure, data, software, and procedures. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring that changes are properly managed and controlled through RBAC policies, admission controllers, and audit logging. RBAC can be used to define who can make changes, admission controllers can enforce policies around changes, and audit logs can track changes. NetworkPolicies can ensure that changes to network configurations are approved and documented.
